audit_items.proof_trace: In processInputBuffer (L1573-1589), after processCommand(c) returns C_OK, the code accesses c->flags, c->read_reploff, c->querybuf, c->qb_pos (L1574-1576) and calls resetClient(c) (L1584) before checking if (server.current_client == NULL) break (L1589). The developer comment at L1586-1588 explicitly acknowledges: 'freeMemoryIfNeeded may flush slave output buffers. This may result into a slave, that may be the active client, to be freed.' The NULL guard is misplaced — it comes after the UAF-prone accesses, not before them. If processCommand(c) returns C_OK while c has been freed (via freeMemoryIfNeeded → flushSlavesOutputBuffers → writeToClient → freeClient(c)), all accesses to c between L1574 and L1584 are use-after-free.
audit_items.attack_scenario: A replica client connected to a Redis master under maxmemory pressure sends REPLCONF ACK; processInputBuffer processes the command with server.current_client = c (the slave); freeMemoryIfNeeded triggers flushSlavesOutputBuffers which calls writeToClient on this same slave, a write failure causes freeClient(c) freeing the client struct; processCommand still returns C_OK, and the subsequent reads/writes to c->flags, c->reploff, and resetClient(c) all access freed heap memory, enabling potential heap corruption.
